name: Jimenez v. Quarterman
question: When a criminal defendant is unable to obtain timely direct review of his case in state court, should the one-year statute of limitations for appeals begin to run at the date of conviction, as federal law prescribes, or after the delayed direct review is completed?
description: A case in which the Court held that if a state court grants a criminal defendant the right to file an out-of-time direct appeal, the statute of limitations doesn't begin until after the out-of-time appeal is final.
